summary: Stunning front‑of‑house analog tape with crystal‑clear soundstage captures a tight, high‑energy 1980 show highlighted by scorching Alabama > Promised Land and a barn‑burning second set.
review: The Bob Wagner FOB recording delivers an almost perfect analog soundstage – the mix is clear, the audience ambience is present but never overwhelms the music, and even onstage conversations are audible. Musically the concert is a showcase of Jerry Garcia’s aggressive “Tiger” tone and inspired playing, with Phil Lesh stepping up his bass work and Brent Mydland adding vibrant organ colors; the setlist flows from a punchy first set into an explosive second half featuring standout moments like Alabama > Promised Land, Fire on the Mountain and a searing Terrapin jam. Overall the performance is described as tight, enthusiastic, and one of the best of the year.

ai_rating.rationale: A single detailed 5‑star review praises both the musical performance and the recording quality; weighting gives a high rating but confidence is limited by only one source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Front‑of‑house analog tape (Bob Wagner FOB) recorded on NAKS CM‑700 and Sony D5; soundstage is described as stunning, clear separation, audience audible but not overbearing, occasional conversation captured adding atmosphere.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A tight, work‑horse first set with Jerry and Bob locked in sync leads into a barn‑burner second set that builds energy through concise gems (Scarlet) to expansive jams (Terrapin, PITB), ending on a powerful Around > Jack close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His new “Tiger” guitar tone is described as aggressive, sharp and explosive; numerous highlights note his inspired licks and vocal peak.
band_member_comments.Phil: Less prominent in the mix early but steps up with strong bass lines, a near‑solo moment during PITB, and pulls notes from older eras.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for tight interplay with Jerry, strong vocals on Terrapin and overall synchronicity throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland gets room to shine on the Leslie organ during AON and adds color throughout; his playing is called “chops”.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as a fast‑paced kettle‑drum barrage that drives the high‑energy jams.
